title: NATO Leader Visits President to Help Friends Get Along
summary: A leader from NATO, a group of countries that work together for safety, met with President Trump at the White House. They talked to try to stop arguments between the U.S. and NATO. The president had said NATO did not help enough in a war. Also, Southwest Airlines is making people pay more money to put bags on planes because fuel costs went up from the war. Other airlines like Delta are doing this too, but some people like soldiers still get free bags. In Georgia, a new person named Clay Fuller will join Congress after winning an election. He supports President Trump and will work in Washington, D.C. Weather experts say last March was very warm, and stock prices went up because people hope for peace in the war.

keywords.explanation: a group of countries that work together to keep peace and safety
the home and office of the U.S. president in Washington, D.C.
money people pay to put their suitcases on an airplane
a group of people in the U.S. government who make laws
a big fight between countries or groups
pieces of companies that people buy and sell, and their prices can go up or down

questions.question: Who met with President Trump at the White House?
Why did Southwest Airlines raise baggage fees?
options: To help with higher fuel costs from the war
To give free bags to everyone
questions.correct_answer: To help with higher fuel costs from the war

questions.question: What did President Trump say about NATO?
options: They were tested and they failed
They helped a lot in the war
questions.correct_answer: They were tested and they failed

questions.question: Who will join Congress from Georgia?
What happened to stock prices because of ceasefire hopes?
Which group still gets free bags on most airlines?

background_read: NATO is like a team of countries that promise to protect each other if there is trouble. The White House is where the U.S. president lives and works. Airlines are companies that fly people in airplanes, and they sometimes charge extra for bags. Congress is part of the U.S. government that makes laws, and people vote to choose who goes there. A war can make things like fuel more expensive, affecting prices. Stocks are like shares in companies that people trade, and their prices change based on news.
Article_Structure: WHO: NATO chief Mark Rutte and President Trump
WHAT: They met to ease tensions between the U.S. and NATO
WHERE: At the White House in Washington, D.C.
WHY: Because the president criticized NATO for not helping in a war and threatened to leave the alliance

perspectives.description: The meeting shows an effort to fix problems and keep the alliance strong.
